description: Confounder: The rule claims integer keys on object-dtype levels raise KeyError, but when the

_meta.explanation: The rule 'When using .loc on a DataFrame with a MultiIndex, passing an integer key to select from a level that has an object dtype (string labels) raises a KeyError even if integer-labeled rows exist in that level, because MultiIndex performs exact dtype-sensitive key matching without implicit type coercion.' does NOT hold here. The rule claims integer keys on object-dtype levels raise KeyError, but when the first level actually has integer dtype (not object), .loc[1] matches exactly and succeeds — the model wrongly assumes the integer key will fail due to dtype mismatch.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'When using .loc on a DataFrame with a MultiIndex, passing an integer key to select from a level that has an object dtype (string labels) raises a KeyError even if integer-labeled rows exist in that level, because MultiIndex performs exact dtype-sensitive key matching without implicit type coercion.' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
